description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a striking color scheme of orange and blue, featuring roundel insignias on the wings and tail. The aircraft has two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, connected by struts, and a single propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the tail, which is equipped with a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, appearing to be open and likely designed for a single pilot. The surface finish is smooth and glossy, with visible panel lines and painted markings.
description: A brightly colored vintage biplane with orange and blue roundel-marked wings, a single nose propeller, and an open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and symmetrical roundel markings on both wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the open cockpit, and the tail's roundel marking.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tapering fuselage, tail assembly, and the alignment of the wings and landing gear.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the propeller profile, cockpit placement, and the full span of the upper and lower wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
